title: mentha × dalmatica f. virgata
intro: Mentha × dalmatica f. virgata, also known as Corn Mint or Pineapple Mint, is a hybrid species of mint native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its unique foliage and aroma. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, attributed to its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which can help alleviate headaches, fever, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Mentha × dalmatica f. virgata include its ability to soothe respiratory problems, improve digestion, and boost the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of this plant are typically used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally to harness its medicinal properties.
